member.office_address: 1226 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4608

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Given the tight deadlines provided by the Appropriations Committee, we will accept programmatic and language requests until March 6 for all subcommittees. We will only be accepting submissions for requests to be submitted by our office directly, that are not part of multi-Member letters. If your request is part of a multi-Member letter, please email the appropriate legislative assistant in our office. Please submit unique requests for each language or programmatic request.

sections.description: Navigation and search controls for the website.

fields.rationale: The account is the Science Mission Directorate, which is the standard 'Science' account within the NASA budget.

fields.label: 7.	Priority Ranking:
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is a user-specific determination.

fields.label: 8.	Will this request be submitted by multiple Members of Congress?
fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values indicate that this specific programmatic request is not led by a multi-member letter, although general support letters for NASA Science do exist.

fields.label: 9. Request Type:
fields.rationale: This is a request for programmatic funding for the Science Mission Directorate account ($9 billion) rather than a request for specific bill or report language.

sections.description: Specific details for requests seeking funding for specific programs.

fields.label: 1.	Program Name:
fields.rationale: This is the specific directorate for which funding is being requested.

fields.label: 2.	Amount desired for FY 2027:
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask amount for FY 2027 as provided in the reference values.

fields.label: 3.	Amount requested in the President's FY2027 Budget:
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released.

fields.label: 4.	Federal funding history (going back five years):
fields.help_text: Please provide numeric dollar amounts for each of the last five fiscal years.
fields.rationale: Providing the most recent enacted funding levels for the Science Mission Directorate as specified in the reference data.

fields.label: 5. One-sentence request summary:
fields.draft_value: This request provides $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to ensure U.S. leadership in space exploration and support over $54 million in local economic activity in VA-8.
fields.rationale: Summarizes the funding goal while highlighting the specific $54.4M economic impact within Representative Beyer's district, including work by local entities like Planet Labs Federal and the Smithsonian Institution.

fields.label: 6. BRIEF description of program value, nationally and to Virginia's 8th Congressional district (500 Character Limit):
fields.draft_value:
* National Leadership: Returns $3 for every $1 invested; ensures the U.S. sets exploration norms and counters rapid Chinese space expansion.
* Local Impact: Over $54M in VA-8 funding (FY23-FY25), supporting Planet Labs ($13.5M) and Northrop Grumman ($2.4M) for Earth science missions.
* Regional Research: Funding for Alexandria's Space Systems Research Corp and Arlington's Smithsonian advances solar weather and planetary discovery.
fields.rationale: Cites $54.4M total district spending and specifically highlights Planet Labs ($13.5M) and Northrop Grumman ($2.4M) as major VA-8 contract recipients. Includes Alexandria-based Space Systems Research Corp and the Smithsonian in Arlington to show broad district-wide scientific impact. Frames national value using the ROI and China competition talking points.

sections.description: Specific details for requests seeking bill or report language changes.

fields.label: 1. Is this Bill or Report Language?
fields.rationale: This section is non-applicable because 'Program' was selected for the Request Type. This request seeks funding for an existing account, not specific language.

fields.label: 2. Please insert your language request here:
fields.rationale: The request context specifies that no specific report or bill language is requested (N/A).

fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request; the language summary field is not applicable.

* Regional Research: Funding for Alexandria's Space Systems Research Corp and Arlington's Smithsonian advances solar weather and planetary discovery.
fields.rationale: Cites $54.4M total district spending and specifically highlights Planet Labs ($13.5M) and Northrop Grumman ($2.4M) as major VA-8 contract recipients. Includes Alexandria-based Space Systems Research Corp and the Smithsonian in Arlington to show broad district-wide scientific impact. top_grants.description: The proposed effort will result in maps of the geology and near-surface properties of the insight landing site. This effort will directly support tactical decisions regarding the placement of seis and hp3  and will characterize the geology of a new location on Mars. Our team members  are experts in analyses of lander data for geologic mapping and constraint of grain properties and are well-equipped to accomplish these objectives  task 1 coincides with the insight deployment phase and will utilize data from the idc and icc cameras to optimize placement of the seis and hp3 instruments in the lander workspace. To achieve this objective  we will map and evaluate the sub-centimeter to decameter scale morphology  local sedimentology  and stratigraphy at the insight landing site. The PI will serve on the isswg and will measure local grain properties and define depositional environments that allow: 1) identifying and characterizing high priority targets in the workspace Digital Terrain Model (DTM); 2) helping interpretation of thermal inertial properties from rad and extrapolating results to the workspace; and 3) constrain the probable distribution of workspace materials to several meters depth to predict materials encountered by hp3. Possible excavations made using the ida scoop will help establish ground truth and soil physical properties (E.g.  To interpret lag deposits that could relate to subsurface properties encountered by hp3). We will rapidly interpret lander data for input into isswg tactical operations and help guide assessment and certification of sites for seis and hp3 deployment. Task 2 largely coincides with the science monitoring phase and the proposed objective will more broadly characterize the geology of landing site via detailed mapping using stereo panorama images from the idc (emphasizing the 360-degree idc panorama if acquired) and icc images collected during both the deployment and science monitoring phases of the mission. We will constrain the geomorphic history of the landing site within the context established prior to landing from orbital mapping. We will also utilize local outcrop and deposit morphology  structure  and grain properties to determine the geologic evolution of the surface and to provide important ground truth for understanding a new  widespread  but poorly understood Terrain: the early hesperian transition unit (EHT) mapped along the dichotomy boundary. Based on pre-landing mapping we anticipate impact  volcanic  and eolian wind processes dominated shaping of the insight landing site  but will also consider contributions by other processes. Monitoring of eolian features to detect potential changes with time will also be analyzed using idc and icc images. The PI grant has experience mapping depositional environments around simple terrestrial craters likely analogous to impact craters and Terrain encountered at the insight landing site. Moreover  the proposal team assists in mapping the changeable settings along the MER and MSL traverses towards understanding setting. Co-i weitz has experience with analysis of grain/rock properties in Mars outcrops and surficial deposits and change detection pertinent to understanding seis and hp3 placement. Collaborator purdy will assist in gis-based mapping during both tasks. The team has a record of success using proposed methods to achieve MER and MSL mission objectives and will do the same for insight. The proposal builds on the PI'S experience in operations as an sowg chair and ltp on the MER and MSL rovers  respectively. Work by co-i weitz builds on her involvement in operations as a surface properties scientist on MSL and analysis of grain size properties on eolian bedforms at gale crater. Collaborator wilson is active in MER and MSL science analyses and was a documentarian on MSL. All are ready to serve in operations roles as members of the isswg and during all insight mission phases in any capacity needed.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Guiding InSight instrument placement through detailed Mars surface mapping.

top_grant_recipients.name: The Institute for Global Environmental Strategies, Inc.
